retry_reason: Failure of refcount_inc_not_zero indicates the socket is being freed or destroyed; retrying on the same object is impossible. The function already retries internally once before returning NULL.
contract_summary: This function performs an RCU-protected lookup of a listening socket in a SAP’s hash table and, if found and still alive, acquires a reference via refcount_inc_not_zero. On success, it returns the socket with an elevated refcount that the caller must later release via sock_put. On failure (NULL), no reference is held. The function safely handles SLAB_TYPESAFE_BY_RCU by retrying internally if the refcount increment fails due to concurrent teardown. The caller must not use the returned socket after calling sock_put, and must not assume the socket is still alive after releasing the RCU read lock.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to acquire a reference (via refcount_inc_not_zero) on a listener socket found via hash lookup. The lookup and match logic is minimal and necessary to locate the object; the core semantics are get-and-return with elevated refcount.
judge.retry_reason: Failure of refcount_inc_not_zero indicates the socket is being freed or destroyed; retrying on the same object is impossible. The function already retries internally once before returning NULL.

check.reason: The get operation via refcount_inc_not_zero is correctly placed on the return value (rc); the path and type chain match. The put operation occurs internally on a socket that is not returned, so it should not be associated with the return value; thus it is removed.
